message:
fix(core): eliminate integer overflow risks and improve borrow patterns across hostcall subsystems

Compaction (compaction.rs):
- Refactor find_cut_point() to use message_from_entry() helper instead of
  direct SessionEntry::Message pattern matching, making the token
  accumulation logic more robust against future entry type additions

AMAC stall telemetry (hostcall_amac.rs):
- Replace .sum() with .fold(0u64, |acc, x| acc.saturating_add(x)) for
  elapsed_ns accumulation to prevent panic on overflow when summing many
  large nanosecond samples
- Apply the same saturating fold pattern to variance computation to prevent
  overflow when squaring large differences

Hostcall queue (hostcall_queue.rs):
- Replace entry().or_insert(0) + saturating_add(1) pattern with explicit
  get_mut/insert to avoid unnecessary String allocation when the key already
  exists in the tenant_backlog map

S3-FIFO policy (hostcall_s3_fifo.rs):
- Apply the same get_mut/insert optimization to increment_owner() to avoid
  cloning the owner string on every increment when the entry already exists
- Refactor decrement_owner() to compute the should_remove decision while
  holding the mutable borrow, then perform the removal after releasing it,
  avoiding a double-lookup pattern

Trace JIT compiler (hostcall_trace_jit.rs):
- Restructure execute_compiled() to evaluate all guards while holding only
  a shared borrow of the cache, extracting tier_improvement_delta and any
  failed guard info before releasing the borrow
- This eliminates the previous pattern where a compiled entry was cloned
  just to avoid borrow conflicts, reducing allocation overhead on the JIT
  hot path
- Guard failure handling now happens after the shared borrow is released,
  allowing record_guard_failure() to take its required mutable borrow
  without conflict

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
